elements.sent-2411-001: Assume that $\mathfrak A$ is a real Banach space of finite dimension $n\geq2$.
elements.sent-2411-002: Consider any Borel probability measure $\nu$ supported on the unit ball $K$ of $\mathfrak A$.
elements.sent-2411-003:
We show that  egin{equation}\Delta(
u)=\int_{x \in K}\int_{ y\in K}|x-y|_{\mathfrak A} \,\,\,
u(x)\,
u(y)\leq 2(1-2^{-n}f(n)),\end{equation} where $f:\mathbb N\setminus \{0,1\}ightarrow (0,1]$ is a concrete universal function such that $f(n)\sim  rac{2 {\mathrm e n^2\log n}$.}
elements.sent-2411-004: It is hoped that in the estimate`$f(n)$' can be replaced by `$1$'.
elements.sent-2411-005: Assume that $\mathfrak A$ is a real Banach space of finite dimension $n$.
elements.sent-2411-006:

u$ is  egin{equation}\Delta(

u(x)\,
u(y),\end{equation} where $|\cdot|_{\mathfrak A}$ denotes the norm on $\mathfrak A$.
elements.sent-2411-007: The trivial estimate is, of course, $\Delta(\nu)\leq2$.
elements.sent-2411-008: This, in some cases, can be approached relatively well:.
elements.sent-2411-009: Assume that $\mathfrak A$ is $\mathbb R^n$ endowed by the maximum norm $|(x_1,\ldots, x_n)|_\infty=\max(|x_1|,\ldots, |x_n|)$; and $\nu$ is distributed uniformly on the vertices $(\pm1,\ldots,\pm1)$ of the unit ball.. Then \begin{equation}\Delta(\nu)=2(1-2^{-n}).\eqedexer\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-010: In what follows we will be interested in estimates for $\Delta(\nu)$ smaller than $2$, depending on the dimension $n$, with emphasis on the asymptotical behaviour.
elements.sent-2411-012: By a theorem of Lassak \cite{L , $K$ can be covered by four of its homothetical copies of ratio $\frac{\sqrt2 {2}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-014: Set $L_1=K\cap K_1$, $L_2=(K\cap K_2)\setminus K_1$, $L_3=(K\cap K_3)\setminus (K_1\cup K_2)$, $L_4=(K\cap K_4)\setminus(K_1\cup K_2\cup K_3)$.. Let $v_i=\nu(L_i)$.
elements.sent-2411-015: For $x,y\in L_i$, we can estimate $|x-y|_{\mathfrak A}$ by $\diam_{\mathfrak A K_i=\sqrt2$, and it can be estimated by $2$ otherwise.. Therefore, \begin{equation}\Delta(\nu)\leq 2(v_1+\ldots+v_4)^2-(2-\sqrt2)( (v_1)^2+\ldots+(v_4)^2 ).\end{equation}.}

elements.sent-2411-022: The higher dimensional case can be treated similarly.
elements.sent-2411-023: Let us recall certain estimates due to Rogers.
elements.sent-2411-024: Let us consider any centrally symmetric compact convex body $H$ in the $n$-dimensional space, $\mathbb R^n$.
elements.sent-2411-025: Let $\Theta(H)$ be the infimum of the covering density of $\mathbb R^n$ by translates of $H$; and let $\Theta_L(H)$ be the same with respect to lattice coverings.
elements.sent-2411-026: Then, by Rogers \cite{R , for $n\geq3$, \begin{equation}\Theta(H)\leq \Theta_L(H)\leq \min_{0<\eta<1/n}(1+\eta)^n(1+n\log(1/\eta))\plabel{eq:r1} < \left(1+\frac1{n\log n}\right)^n(1+n\log(n\log n))\plabel{eq:r2} < n\log n +n\log\log n+2n+1 \plabel{eq:r3} < n\log n +n\log\log n+5n.\plabel{eq:r4}\end{equation} Here \eqref{eq:r1 is the proper result of \cite{R (which can be expressed explicitly using the Lambert $W_{-1}$ function); \eqref{eq:r2 reflects the choice $\eta=\frac1{n\log n}$; and while, for example, \eqref{eq:r3 is still true (but we do no want to spend time on it), \eqref{eq:r4 is the generally quoted estimate; see G.}}}}}}
elements.formula-2411-008: \begin{equation}\Theta(H)\leq \Theta_L(H)\leq \min_{0<\eta<1/n}(1+\eta)^n(1+n\log(1/\eta))\plabel{eq:r1} < \left(1+\frac1{n\log n}\right)^n(1+n\log(n\log n))\plabel{eq:r2} < n\log n +n\log\log n+2n+1 \plabel{eq:r3} < n\log n +n\log\log n+5n.\plabel{eq:r4}\end{equation}
